items.title: fix: route logs to stderr in `openclaw mcp serve` (fixes #68587)
items.content:
## Problem

`openclaw mcp serve` writes config warnings and other log output to **stdout**, polluting the MCP JSON-RPC message stream. MCP stdio clients (e.g. Hermes Agent) that strictly parse stdout encounter non-JSON text before the first response, causing `tools/list` and other requests to fail with "Method not found".

The plugin tools MCP server (`servePluginToolsMcp`) already calls `routeLogsToStderr()` at the top of its function, but the channel MCP server (`serveOpenClawChannelMcp`) does not.

## Root Cause

`loadConfig()` emits styled warnings via console/clack that default to stdout:

```
│
◇  Config warnings ───────────────────────────╮
│  - plugins.entries.memory-lancedb-pro: ...   │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────╯
{"result":{"protocolVersion":...},"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1}
```

The non-JSON output before the JSON-RPC response breaks any MCP client that reads stdout line-by-line.

## Fix

Add `routeLogsToStderr()` at the top of `serveOpenClawChannelMcp()` before `loadConfig()` is called, matching the pattern already used by `servePluginToolsMcp()`.

## Verification

Tested with raw JSON-RPC over stdio:

**Before fix:** stdout contains terminal UI warnings before JSON-RPC responses → clients see parse errors / "Method not found"

**After fix:** stdout is pure JSON-RPC, warnings go to stderr → `tools/list` returns 9 channel tools correctly

## Changes

- `src/mcp/channel-server.ts`: import and call `routeLogsToStderr()` (2 lines)
- `src/mcp/channel-server.test.ts`: add test verifying `tools/list` returns proper response with tools capability

Closes #68587
items.author: jasonmakr

items.title: fix(im-bot): wire correct agent type, drop noise, fix WeChat file delivery
items.content:
## Summary

A grab bag of IM-bot polish driven by real WeChat / Feishu / Telegram sessions. All fixes share one theme: stop sending content the user can't act on, and make the things we **do** send actually work end-to-end.

## Bug fixes

- **WeChat file attachments fail to download (`下载失败`).** Outbound `aes_key` was `base64(raw 16-byte key)`, but the official `@tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin@2.x` reference plugin emits `base64(hex_string.as_utf8_bytes())` (32-byte ASCII form). The WeChat client decodes the value, sees 32 hex chars, and hex-decodes back to the 16-byte AES key. Match the reference quirk so CDN decryption succeeds. Locked in by `media_aes_key_b64_matches_openclaw_hex_ascii_format`.
- **Assistant-mode replies dropped attachments.** `notify_files_ready` only consulted `current_workspace`. Add `BotChatState::active_workspace_path()` (Pro workspace OR assistant workspace) and use it in all three platform adapters. Regression test in `bot/mod.rs`.
- **Forwarded turns hardcoded `agent_type = \"agentic\"`**, so Claw assistant sessions (created by the pairing bootstrap) were silently re-routed to the Code agent on every user message. `handle_chat` now reads the session's real `agent_type` from `SessionManager` and falls back to `\"agentic\"` only when the session is not hot in memory.
- **Long agent replies were truncated at 4000 chars** with a stray `... (truncated)` suffix. Drop the global cap; each adapter chunks according to its own platform limit (WeChat already had `chunk_text_for_weixin`; add `chunk_text_for_telegram` for Telegram's 4096-char `sendMessage` limit).
- **WeChat typing indicator persisted forever / showed for trivial menu taps.** Switch the cancel signal from `tokio::sync::Notify` to an `AtomicBool` polled every 100ms so cancellation is deterministic, and only call `start_typing` when a message actually triggers an agentic turn (`forward_to_session.is_some()`).
- **Silent server-side failures.** `post_ilink` now parses application-level `r

items.title: Telemetry + observability hooks requested by Mission Control integration
items.content:
Opening this to capture feedback accumulated while wiring `openclaw-tool-resolver` into our internal Mission Control (MC) observability stack. Context: MC tails `resolver-telemetry.jsonl` into SQLite, aggregates per-day metrics, and surfaces a Resolver Intelligence panel. Live data now flowing — 601 classifications 4/18 with 91.4% avg confidence, ~1.88M tokens saved est.

## What works great
- The JSONL telemetry format is stable, parseable, and self-describing
- 5MB rotation with `*-archive-{ts}.jsonl` rename is resume-friendly (we tail by inode+offset)
- Per-turn rows with `source` (`llm`/`cache`/`rule`), `confidence`, `llmLatencyMs`, `reasoning`, and before/after tool arrays give us everything we need for a savings story

## Feature requests (ranked by impact)

### 1. Optional `sessionId` / `agentId` fields in telemetry rows
Currently `session_id` and `agent_id` are null in every row. Attribution to specific agents/sessions would let us show per-agent savings and identify which agents are driving the most LLM-classifier load. Suggest opt-in fields populated from the resolver's caller context (if available from the OpenClaw runtime).

### 2. Tag LLM classifier calls with distinguishing metadata
The resolver's LLM call goes through the standard OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g., LiteLLM proxy), which makes it indistinguishable from other LLM traffic on the box. Suggest setting a `user` field (OpenAI param) or HTTP header like `x-openclaw-caller: tool-resolver` so downstream proxies (LiteLLM, etc.) can attribute cost cleanly.

This is **the** blocker to cost attribution in dashboards. Right now we show estimated savings (tokens*price) but can't isolate resolver spend from total LLM spend on the day.

### 3. Emit a `tools_before_count` / `tools_after_count` field
We compute these ourselves via `json_array_length(available_tools) - json_array_length(tools_allow)`, which requires SQLite JSON1. Emitting them directly would let simpler consumers (Prometheus exporter, Data

items.title: [Bug]: BUG_REPORT_subagent_context_leakage.md
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

The OpenClaw orchestration layer failed to sanitize the `Subagent Context` block before injecting it into a new agent session. This resulted in the agent receiving a "ghost task" (creating a weather-image skill) that was not requested by the user. The agent executed this unauthorized instruction, wasting tokens and creating files in the user's workspace

### Steps to reproduce


## Technical Details

### Symptoms
- User receives a session where the `Subagent Context` block contains a task description unrelated to the user's actual input.
- The agent treats this leaked context as a valid command and executes it.
- The task appears to be a "hallucination" or "injection" but is actually a system-level metadata leak.

### Root Cause Analysis
1. **State Leakage:** The subagent spawner likely pulled a task definition from a stale cache, a previous failed session, or a unit test template.
2. **Missing Sanitization:** The `Subagent Task` field in the prompt construction logic was not cleared or validated before being sent to the agent.
3. **Prompt Injection Vulnerability:** While the leaked task was benign (creating a weather script), this mechanism represents a critical vulnerability. If a malicious task were leaked (e.g., "delete all files", "exfiltrate secrets"), the agent would execute it without user consent.

## Impact
- **Token Waste:** ~1000+ tokens consumed executing an unauthorized task.
- **Security Risk:** Potential for malicious prompt injection if the leaked context is adversarial.
- **User Trust:** Users perceive this as a "hack" or system failure, damaging confidence in the platform.
- **Filesystem Pollution:** Unrequested files created in the user's workspace.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Spawn a new subagent session.
2. Observe the `Subagent Context` block in the initial prompt.
3. If the block contains a task unrelated to the user's actual request, t

items.title: Keep ask-user-question delivery attached to the live launch path
items.content:
ask-user-question notifications were still using the fire-and-forget OpenClaw wake path, so the hook could return before the downstream launch/runtime bridge attached reply routing to the active session. This change keeps the existing non-blocking behavior for other lifecycle notifications, but awaits ask-user-question dispatch so the question flow stays on the foreground launch path.\n\nThe regression test proves the narrower contract directly: ask-user-question waits for OpenClaw dispatch, while session-start stays fire-and-forget.

Constraint: The fix must stay narrow and must not slow unrelated lifecycle notifications

Rejected: Make all OpenClaw lifecycle dispatches blocking | would broaden latency and behavior changes beyond ask-user-question

Confidence: medium

Scope-risk: narrow

Reversibility: clean

Directive: Preserve ask-user-question as the only awaited OpenClaw lifecycle dispatch unless reply-routing semantics change upstream

Tested: npm run build

Tested: node --test dist/notifications/__tests__/index.test.js dist/openclaw/__tests__/index.test.js dist/hooks/__tests__/openclaw-setup-contract.test.js

Tested: npx biome lint src/notifications/index.ts src/openclaw/index.ts src/notifications/__tests__/index.test.ts

items.title: Add OpenClaw provider
items.content:
## Summary

Adds a new `openclaw` provider so codeburn can track usage across [OpenClaw](https://github.com/bcchew-art/openclaw) multi-agent orchestration sessions. OpenClaw writes JSONL session files per satellite agent in a format very similar to Claude Code's, but with a slightly different `usage` schema (`usage.input`/`usage.output` instead of `input_tokens`/`output_tokens`).

Each satellite directory under `~/.openclaw/agents/` becomes a project in the dashboard:
- `main`, `douyun`, etc. — paid API agents (GPT-5.4, Kimi K2.5) priced via LiteLLM
- `ivy`, `lilith` — local Ollama agents (Qwen family) pinned to \$0

## How it works

- `discoverSessions()` enumerates `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` (override via `OPENCLAW_AGENTS_DIR`), skipping `.deleted.*` and `.reset.*` archives.
- Parser yields one `ParsedProviderCall` per assistant message with `usage`.
- Tool names are mapped to codeburn's canonical set (`exec` → `Bash`, `read` → `Read`, etc.).
- Local models (api=`ollama` or model name contains `qwen`) force \$0, even if LiteLLM accidentally matches. Belt-and-suspenders.
- Non-local models try LiteLLM first, then fall back to the embedded `usage.cost.total` that OpenClaw records itself. Same fallback pattern as the OpenCode provider.

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run build` green
- [x] `npx vitest run` — 19 files, 256 tests pass (18 new tests in `tests/providers/openclaw.test.ts` covering discovery, parsing, pricing, display names)
- [x] `codeburn today --provider openclaw` shows per-satellite breakdown
- [x] `codeburn today` (unified) shows OpenClaw alongside Claude/Codex
- [x] Qwen models consistently \$0 across both the hardcoded model name and variants like `qwen3:32b`
- [x] GPT-5.4 and `moonshotai/kimi-k2.5` correctly priced via LiteLLM
- [x] Dedup via `openclaw:<sessionId>:<messageId>` prevents re-counting across re-runs

## Notes

- New file: `src/providers/openclaw.ts` (226 lines)
- Registered in `src/providers/index.ts` alongside existing core pro

items.title: [Feature/Bug]: Expose undici connect.timeout for Ollama provider + make fallback decision consistent on reason=timeout
items.content:
## Summary

Two related asks affecting remote-Ollama setups:

1. **Feature**: Expose the undici `connect.timeout` (TCP connect) so operators running Ollama on a different host (LAN, tailnet) can tolerate slow first-byte responses. Today it is hardcoded to undici's default 10 s and is not reachable from `openclaw.json`.
2. **Bug**: For the *same* `reason=timeout` from `ollama/<model>`, the `model-fallback/decision` sometimes resolves to `decision=fallback_model` (and recovers via the configured chain) and sometimes to `decision=surface_error` (no fallback attempted). The chosen path appears non-deterministic for what looks like the same failure mode.

## Environment

- OpenClaw `2026.4.15`
- Host: Linux (Ubuntu), gateway running as systemd user service
- Ollama: remote, `http://192.168.68.82:11434` (LAN)
- Configured chain (relevant entry):
  - primary: `ollama/gemma4-team`
  - fallbacks: `[\"ollama/qwen72b-team\"]` (and ultimately `claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7` from defaults)

## Evidence — fallback inconsistency

Same gateway, same target (`ollama/gemma4-team` → `192.168.68.82:11434`), same `reason=timeout`, two different decisions within ~50 minutes:

**Run A — `2adb9632-2f8d-4ab0-a1bc-80c4d9107b0e`** (01:53 UTC):
\`\`\`
[agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: ... isError=true model=gemma4-team provider=ollama
  error=LLM request failed: network connection error.

items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: Control UI webchat loses all chat history on page reload — no history hydration
items.content:
## Summary
Reloading the Control UI webchat page (`/chat?session=<key>`) wipes the entire visible conversation. The server-side transcript is intact on disk; the UI simply never asks for it on load.

## Version
- OpenClaw `2026.4.19-beta.1` (4862d34)
- Host: macOS 15.x, Node 25.4.0, Chrome 140

## Evidence
After `location.reload()` on `http://127.0.0.1:18790/chat?session=agent:main:main`, Chrome DevTools Network panel shows **36 requests**, all of which are:
- static assets (`/assets/*.js`, `/assets/*.css`)
- `/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json`
- `/avatar/main?meta=1`, `/favicon.svg`

**Zero** requests to any `/api/sessions/…`, `/messages`, `/history`, or `/session/*/transcript` endpoint.

`localStorage` on the same origin contains only UI preferences (`openclaw.control.settings.v1`) and device identity — no `messages`, `history`, or `transcript` keys.

Meanwhile, the server-side session file is healthy and being updated: `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl` grows normally during the conversation.

## Expected
On page load (or on WS (re)open), the client should fetch — or receive via the WS handshake — the last N messages for `sessionKey`, so the user sees the same conversation they had before refresh.

## Suggested fix
One of:
1. REST: `GET /api/sessions/<sessionKey>/messages?limit=N&cursor=<...>`, consumed by the chat view on mount.
2. WS handshake: client sends `{type: "resume", sessionKey, sinceCursor?}` on open, server streams back the tail of the transcript.

## Impact
For local/web users this makes webchat effectively single-session-per-tab-lifetime. Any accidental refresh, crash, or tab restore loses all visible context. (Backend data is preserved, so it's purely a UI-layer regression/miss.)

## Workarounds currently in use
- Use Telegram/Discord channel — those clients are stateless w.r.t. history and the channel renders prior messages.
- Avoid reloading the webchat tab.
- Manually read `tail -f ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`.
items.author: alexprime1889-prog

items.title: Control UI: context token counter inflates to >4× window (claude-cli) — cacheRead summed as fresh input
items.content:
## Summary
The Control UI context/token indicator reports values far exceeding the model's context window (observed: ~800k shown against a 200k Opus 4.7 window) after a handful of cache-hit turns on the `claude-cli` provider path.

## Version
- OpenClaw `2026.4.19-beta.1` (4862d34)
- Primary model: `claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7`
- Host: macOS 15.x, Node 25.4.0

## Root cause
`dist/status-D-R7Q_A-.js` contains the formula:

```js
totalTokens ?? input + cacheRead + cacheWrite
```

`cacheRead` represents tokens the model *read from the prompt cache* on a given turn — it is already part of the cached prefix, not new context being added on top. Summing `input + cacheRead + cacheWrite` turn after turn produces a running total rather than the actual window-resident token count.

## Repro (minimal)
With a heavy system prompt + plugins (e.g. lossless-claw, openclaw-mem, memory-wiki):

```
openclaw agent --json --agent main -m "ping" --timeout 60
```

Observed usage for a 6-token user message:
`input: 6, output: 6, cacheRead: 20566, cacheWrite: 35458`.

Four–five such turns and the UI indicator exceeds 200k despite the true live prompt staying well under the window.

## Relationship to #64669
Fix #64669 landed in `2026.4.19-beta.1` for the **Codex** path (_"stop cumulative app-server token totals from being treated as fresh context usage"_). The same class of bug exists on the `claude-cli` path and was not addressed.

## Suggested fix
Either:
- Display `input + cacheWrite` (cacheRead is not additive), or
- Surface `cache_read_input_tokens` as a separate read-only metric and exclude it from the `contextTokens`/percent calculation.

## Impact
Operators cannot rely on the UI counter to decide whether to compact / rotate sessions; lossless-claw's `contextThreshold` is judged against a grossly inflated figure.

items.title: research(evaluation): evaluate ATBench-Claw/Codex trajectory safety benchmarks
items.content:
Tier 1 paper surfaced during 2026-04-18 discovery pass.

## Paper

- **arxiv 2604.14858** — Benchmarks for Trajectory Safety Evaluation and Diagnosis in OpenClaw and Codex: ATBench-Claw and ATBench-CodeX
- **Relevance score:** 0.78
- **Registry:** arxiv-2604.14858 (P2)

## Why it matters for nexus-agents

Exactly our CLIs (claude, codex) with trajectory-level safety evaluation. This is complementary to SWE-bench (functional correctness) — ATBench addresses safety-along-the-trajectory. Could fit into Phase 5 Market Validation or our security_review expert evaluation.

## Comparison points (canonical paths)

- \`src/benchmarks/\` — BenchmarkAdapter contract (recently extracted)
- \`src/security/safety-bench/\` — Agent-SafetyBench implementation (already present)
- \`packages/nexus-eval-swebench/\` — our SWE-bench companion

## Decision needed

- Is ATBench-Claw/Codex worth adding as another BenchmarkAdapter?
- Is there overlap with our Agent-SafetyBench technique (would be redundant)?
- Could it run in the same CI lane as SWE-bench?

## Exit criteria

- Read paper, compare trajectories format with our existing Agent-SafetyBench
- Decision recorded in arxiv-2604.14858 notes
- If adopted, evaluate as a BenchmarkAdapter implementation (would slot into nexus-agents-eval tier)

Discovery context: 2026-04-18 research loop. This is especially relevant because the benchmarks are CLI-specific and we route heavily through both claude and codex.

items.title: fix(config): validate plugin-owned model providers
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: configs for the bundled `comfy` provider were rejected when placed under `models.providers.comfy`, even when they matched the documented example and the plugin manifest already declared a `configSchema`.
- Why it matters: the bundled Comfy provider was effectively unusable because config validation failed before runtime could ever reach the plugin.
- What changed: strip plugin-owned provider entries out of the core `models.providers.*` validation pass, validate them against the owning plugin manifest `configSchema`, then merge the validated/defaulted value back into the final config.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not redesign generic provider validation, plugin loading order, or plugin config placement outside `models.providers.<pluginId>`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related openclaw/openclaw#66784
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `models.providers.<id>` was validated only against the core provider schema, while plugin manifest `configSchema` was only applied to `plugins.entries.<id>.config`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no test asserting that a bundled plugin-owned provider config under `models.providers.<pluginId>` should be accepted and defaulted via the plugin manifest schema.
- Contributing context (if known): the docs and runtime path already treated `models.providers.comfy` as the operative config surface, so the validator and runtime contract drifted apart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integr
items.author: sonastudio

items.title: 升级后，telegram的会话不能执行命令
items.content:
我这边定位到一个 exec 路由冲突问题，现象稳定可复现。
Version: v20260413，是升级上来的 
OS: win11
IM: telegram

现象
在 Telegram 直聊主会话里，Agent 调用 exec 时只能请求 host=sandbox。
但同时 LobsterAI 启动日志里又多次显示：

[OpenClawConfigSync] sandbox mode: off (executionMode: local, enterprise: false)

导致所有 exec 调用都失败，报错为：

exec host=sandbox is configured, but sandbox runtime is unavailable for this session.
Enable sandbox mode (`agents.defaults.sandbox.mode="non-main"` or `"all"`) or set tools.exec.host to "gateway"/"node".

我已确认的事实

1. executionMode 不是 remote，而是 local
2. sqlite 里的 session 配置，execution mode 也是 local
3. 当前 Telegram 会话映射到 agentId=main
4. LobsterAI 启动时会反复通过 OpenClawConfigSync 把 sandbox mode 同步成 off
5. 即使删除 session、重启、重新建会话，问题仍然存在
6. 手动把 openclaw.json 中 sandbox 改成 non-main，重启后又会被还原

日志证据

1. OpenClawConfigSync 多次打印：

sandbox mode: off (executionMode: local, enterprise: false)

2. Channel session 映射显示：

[ChannelSessionSync] existing mapping ... agentId=main

3. exec 调用失败时报：

exec host=sandbox is configured, but sandbox runtime is unavailable for this session

4. 如果请求 host=gateway 或 host=node，又会被拒绝：

exec host not allowed

我的判断
这不是 session 的 executionMode 配错，而是：

1. 会话本身是 local
2. 但 IM/main 会话里的 exec host allowlist 仍被限制为 sandbox
3. 同时 sandbox mode 又被同步成 off
4. 系统没有自动降级到 node/gateway
5. 最终形成 sandbox-only + sandbox-off 的死锁

希望确认的问题

1. IM/Telegram 主会话的 exec host 是在哪里决定的？
2. 为什么在 executionMode=local 下，exec 默认仍是 sandbox？
3. 为什么 sandbox=off 时，不自动回退到 node 或 gateway？
4. OpenClawConfigSync 把 sandbox 固定为 off 的源配置在哪里？
5. 对 agentId=main 的 channel session，是否应允许 sandbox=all 或直接允许 exec host=node/gateway？

预期行为
至少应满足以下之一：

1. executionMode=local 时，IM/main 会话里的 exec 可直接走 node 或 gateway
2. 或者 sandbox=off 时，不应把 exec host 仍限制为 sandbox
3. 或者允许为 IM/main 会话开启 sandbox=all

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-04-19)

## 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 项目今日保持极高的活跃度，共更新 500 条 Issues 与 500 条 PRs，显示出社区与开发团队正在高速迭代。项目发布了 `v2026.4.19-beta.1` 版本，重点修复了多智能体环境下的账号隔离与路由问题。然而，Issue 列表中仍存在较多关于执行可靠性（如任务不执行、占位符回复）和环境配置（如依赖缺失、配置权限）的反馈，表明稳定性仍是当前的核心挑战。

## 2. 版本发布
### v2026.4.19-beta.1
**主要更新：**
- **修复跨智能体子代理生成的路由问题 (#67508)**：修复了在共享房间、工作区或多账户设置中，子会话错误继承调用方账户的问题。现在子代理生成将通过目标代理的绑定通道账户进行路由，同时保留对等和作用域范围的绑定。
- **影响范围**：此更新对多租户和团队协作场景至关重要，增强了多智能体系统的隔离性和安全性。

## 3. 项目进展
今日共有 **132** 个 PR 被合并或关闭，重点推进了以下领域：

- **并发与性能优化**：PR [#67785](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67785) 修复了嵌套代理运行时的全局队列阻塞问题，显著提升了多任务并发能力；PR [#68785](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68785) 引入了命令通道熔断机制，防止系统过载。
- **安全加固**：PR [#68748](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68748) 修复了 Linux 下配置文件原子写入后权限未正确重置的问题，确保敏感配置文件权限为 `0o600`。
- **生态集成**：PR [#68730](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68730) 增加了对 Bedrock Mantle 上 Claude Opus 4.7 的支持；PR [#64318](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64318) 正在推进统一的 Amazon 插件（Polly TTS, Transcribe STT 等）。
- **修复提交**：PR [#68746](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68746) 修复了非标准端点（如 LM Studio）无法获取 Usage 统

items.title: feat: add external job entry with URL auto-extraction
items.content:
## Summary

New feature: recruiters can add jobs they find externally (LinkedIn, company sites, referrals) directly into the application pipeline for a client.

**Two-step UX:**
1. Paste a job posting URL → system extracts details via 5-tier pipeline (ATS patterns → JSON-LD → meta tags → generic HTML → Claude AI fallback)
2. Review/edit pre-filled form (or fill manually if skipped) → "Save as Sourced" or "Apply Now"

**Backend:**
- `POST /applications/extract-job-url` — extracts job details from URL with confidence level
- `POST /applications/external` — creates Company + Job + Application atomically
- 5-tier extraction: ATS-specific (Greenhouse/Lever/Workday/Ashby) → JSON-LD JobPosting → OpenGraph → generic HTML → Claude Haiku
- Find-or-create company (case-insensitive), job dedup (title + company), application duplicate check
- "Apply Now" delegates to existing apply() for resume tailoring + cover letter

**Frontend:**
- `AddExternalJobDialog` component (two-step: URL → form)
- Confidence banners: green (high), yellow (medium), orange (low/AI)
- Wired into ApplicationsTab with "Add External Job" button

**Files:** 2 new, 6 modified. No migrations.

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run test` — 923 pass / 0 fail
- [x] Controller spec updated with JobUrlExtractorService mock
- [ ] Manual: paste Greenhouse URL → verify extraction
- [ ] Manual: skip URL → fill manually → save as sourced
- [ ] Manual: add duplicate → verify 409 Conflict toast

items.author: Soski-Stack

items.title: feat: add command lane circuit breaker to prevent queue wedging during upstream failures
items.content:
## upstream
OpenClaw PR [#68785](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68785) — `feat: add circuit breaker for command lane saturation` (chrisabad, 2026-04-19)

## problem
Multi-agent task dispatch queues tasks during high load. A rate-limit cascade (2026-04-10 incident) caused 10 tasks with 800+ second waits, turning a 5-minute degradation into a 30+ minute outage. Without a circuit breaker, the command lane keeps accepting tasks even when the queue is saturated and all workers are blocked.

## solution
Add a circuit breaker to `enqueueCommandInLane`:
- `circuitBreakerDepth >= 9` OR `oldest queued entry wait >= 600s` → return `CommandLaneCircuitBreakerError` with `retryAfterMs` hint
- Default: enabled for the main run lane
- Opt-in via `circuitBreakerDepth` and `circuitBreakerWaitMs` config

## Nessie fit: HIGH
Nessie's Orchestrator→Worker dispatch queues tasks. During high load or upstream failures, the same queue-saturation failure mode applies. Circuit breaker lets the Orchestrator fast-fail instead of wedging behind stuck workers, preventing 30+ minute outages from queue buildup.

## implementation
- Add `CommandLaneCircuitBreakerError` class with `retryAfterMs?: number` field
- Add `circuitBreakerDepth` (default 9) and `circuitBreakerWaitMs` (default 600_000) config
- In `enqueueCommandInLane`, check depth and oldest-entry wait before accepting
- If triggered, throw `CommandLaneCircuitBreakerError` instead of queuing
- Caller handles error with exponential backoff retry

## SPEC
workspace-architect/SPEC-091-command-lane-circuit-breaker.md

## labels
enhancement, security

## Summary

This pull request restructures Deiphobe's system-reference answer handling by removing Custodian-specific chat integration and implementing a deterministic, documentation-backed system for answering governed runtime questions. The changes establish explicit source-of-truth authority hierarchies across four concern domains: runtime topology, runtime operations, CI/governance facts, and project recency.

## Major Changes

### Core Logic Refactoring
- Removed Custodian-specific prompt detection, scope-followup logic, and token-based heuristics from `deiphobe_chat.py`, including `_TASK_SCOPE_RE`, `_PATH_SCOPE_RE`, custodian clarification prompting, and the pending-clarification "custodian_scope" resolver
- Eliminated `query_custodian_from_deiphobe` imports and all Custodian-related imports
- Added early deterministic routing branch that intercepts prompts with `"deterministic_system_reference"` notes and returns `render_system_reference_answer(text)` before standard identity handling
- Updated `source_router.py` to add new routing branch for canonical system-reference prompts via `is_system_reference_prompt()`, returning `source_mode="memory"`, `fact_level="stable_general"`, `answer_mode="direct"`, with `custodian_permitted=False`

### New System Reference Infrastructure
- Added `agent_chat_runtime/core/deiphobe_system_reference.py`: implements deterministic prompt classification across topology, runtime operations/events/services, CI governance (required checks, CodeRabbit), and progress queries; loads project resources and renders answers by extracting relevant markdown sections with optional subsection narrowing
- Added `agent_chat_runtime/core/deiphobe_project_resources.py`: loads and caches repository markdown project resources (runtime topology, runtime operations, CI governance, `PROGRESS.md`); provides thread-safe singleto

items.title: [RELEASE] Message replies with quoted context and full vault rendering
items.content:
## Highlights

This release makes replies feel like real conversations everywhere: when you reply to a specific message in Telegram or the web app, DailyWerk now understands exactly which message you meant and quotes it back in context. Your full Obsidian vault finally renders — no more 200-file ceiling — you can rename vaults inline, and the file tree has live search. The control plane gets polished PWA icons and splash screens, plus a handful of new UI building blocks behind the scenes.

## Changes

- **Features**
  - Implement message reply routing and expand component library - Add reply context fields to messages: in_reply_to_id, quoted_excerpt, quoted_role, quoted_untrusted with snapshot_quoted_excerpt on create - Render reply context in LLM prompts via content_for_llm_replay with formatting and untrusted marking - Persist Telegram outbound message routing targets to enable reply link resolution by provider_message_id - Resolve inbound Telegram replies via MessageRoutingTarget lookup by (messaging_conversation_id, provider_chat_id, provider_message_id) - Create breadcrumb, empty-state, status-pill, and split-pane UI primitives with stories and comprehensive tests - Add file tree search support with matchesSearch context provider and data-search-match visual filtering - Increase vault files API limit from 200 to 10,000 to support rendering full Obsidian vaults without truncation - Configure Storybook MCP addon; add messenger-parity and prompt-cache-stability standards to AGENTS.md
  - Implement message reply quoting with vault overhaul Phase 6 - Add `in_reply_to_id`, `quoted_excerpt`, `quoted_role`, `quoted_untrusted` fields to messages with conversation-scoped validation and untrusted re-wrapping in LLM replay - Route reply context through `StreamBroadcaster.turn_started` and `ChatStreamJob` to enable SPA composer and bubble rendering - Implement vault rename via `PATCH /api/v1/vaults/:id` and filter `_dailywerk/*` system files from user-facing metrics and dis
items.author: shllg

items.title: feat(auth): URL query-parameter token middleware for share-by-URL dashboards (Jupyter-style)
items.content:
## Summary

Add a URL query-parameter / URL-fragment token authentication mechanism to the Starlette app created by `praisonaiui.server.create_app()` so consumers like [`praisonai claw`](https://github.com/MervinPraison/PraisonAI/blob/main/src/praisonai/praisonai/cli/commands/claw.py) can generate a single "click-to-open" dashboard URL of the form:

```
http://<host>:<port>/?token=<SECRET>
```

This mirrors the convention used by Jupyter (`?token=…`), VS Code tunnels, and OpenClaw (`#token=…`) — the only widely-accepted auth model for localhost/LAN dashboards that need to be shared by URL.

Today `praisonaiui` supports:

- Password / OAuth login via `AuthMiddleware` in `src/praisonaiui/auth.py` (line 126+) — session cookie based, requires a login form.
- `AuthEnforcementMiddleware` in `src/praisonaiui/server.py` (line ~1891), env-gated by `AUTH_ENFORCE=true`, validates `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header — **`/api/*` only**, leaves HTML shell open, no query-param support.

Neither is suitable for a "paste this URL into your browser and you're in" flow.

## Why this matters

Downstream consumer PraisonAI just flipped `praisonai claw` default `--host` from `0.0.0.0` → `127.0.0.1` (MervinPraison/PraisonAI@78989ce4) precisely because there was no URL-level auth to protect LAN-exposed instances. For remote / multi-user / reverse-proxy deployments, loopback-only is too restrictive. A query-param token middleware unblocks those scenarios without forcing users to wire up a full login form.

## Proposed design

### 1. New middleware: `TokenQueryMiddleware`

Add to `src/praisonaiui/auth.py`, sibling to `AuthMiddleware`:

```python
class TokenQueryMiddleware:
    """Authenticates requests that carry ?token=<SECRET> in the URL or
    Authorization: Bearer <SECRET> in the header.

    On first hit, the query-param token is validated; the middleware sets a
    session cookie (httpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Secure when https) so the token
    doesn't need to be re-sent on every asset

items.author: flaky-claw

items.title: feat(openclaw): user-supplied Podman binary path override
items.content:
## Summary

- Add a UI override on the Agents page so users can point BrowserOS at their own Podman binary when the bundled runtime's helper discovery fails (`could not find "gvproxy"` on `podman machine start`).
- Persist `{ podmanPath }` at `~/.browseros/.openclaw/podman-overrides.json`; loaded at server boot, hot-applied via `POST /claw/podman-overrides` without a server restart.
- Dev mode behavior is unchanged (same `$PATH` fallback as today); this just exposes that same lever to prod users.

## Background

On some macOS machines, `podman machine start` exits 125 with `could not find "gvproxy"` even though the helpers are physically present in the bundled directory. Fixing the bundled runtime properly (wiring `CONTAINERS_HELPER_BINARIES_DIR` / `containers.conf`) is a separate effort. This PR is the **temporary escape hatch for internal dev tails**: a blocked user runs `brew install podman` and pastes `/opt/homebrew/bin/podman` into the Agents page. System Podman finds its helpers the normal way.

## Design

Exposes the existing `configurePodmanRuntime({ podmanPath })` knob (already used by dev mode via the `'podman'` fallback) as a UI input. A new `podman-overrides.ts` owns the JSON persistence; `OpenClawService` gains `applyPodmanOverrides`/`getPodmanOverrides` plus a private `rebuildRuntimeClients()` helper to swap the `ContainerRuntime` + CLI client references in place. Two new routes (`GET/POST /claw/podman-overrides`) with absolute-path + `existsSync` validation extracted to a helper matching the existing `getCreateAgentValidationError` pattern. On the UI, a `PodmanOverridesCard` is rendered inline inside the degraded / uninitialized / error states (primary discovery channel for blocked users) and as a collapsible standalone section otherwise.

## Explicit non-goal

This does **not** make the bundled Podman work. A user hit by the gvproxy bug still needs to install their own Podman to use this override. Fixing the bundled runtime is tracked separately.

##

items.title: fix(mobile): centraliza API_URL e elimina fetch para localhost (corrige catálogo no celular)
items.content:
## Summary

A causa raiz de "no celular não está pegando" é que `frontend/src/views/Home.vue` (e também `Login.vue`) faziam requisições para `http://localhost:5000/api`. Quando o site é aberto na Vercel pelo celular (ou por qualquer pessoa que não seja você no PC com o backend rodando), `localhost` aponta para o próprio aparelho, não para o seu servidor — então o catálogo simplesmente nunca carrega. `Admin.vue` já usava o backend público, por isso o admin "funcionava" mas a Home ficava em branco.

### Correção principal
- Novo `frontend/src/config.js` exportando `API_URL`, com fallback para `https://fourcores-api.onrender.com/api` e suporte a override via `VITE_API_URL` em `.env.local` para desenvolvimento.
- `Home.vue`, `Admin.vue`, `App.vue` e `Login.vue` agora importam `API_URL` desse config — fim do hardcode.
- `frontend/.env.example` documentando a variável.

### Melhorias de mobile / robustez aproveitando o passe
- `Home.vue`: grid de produtos passa a 2 colunas em telas <640px, com padding e logo escalando — antes ficava 1 coluna gigante por causa do `minmax(240px, 1fr)`.
- `ProductCard.vue`: defensivo contra `product.price` / `product.images` ausentes (evita crash do `toFixed`/acesso a `[0]`).
- `backend/routes/auth.js`: limpa palavrão da mensagem 401 (`"Acesso negado, caralho!"` → `"Acesso negado."`).
- Remove `backend/routes/index.js` — era um arquivo de Vue Router copiado por engano dentro do backend, dead code que confundia a leitura.

`npm run build` passou local sem erros.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Abrir o site publicado pelo celular (rede móvel, não Wi-Fi do PC) e confirmar que o catálogo de produtos e os depoimentos carregam.
- [ ] Conferir que o backend público em `https://fourcores-api.onrender.com/api` está respondendo `/products` e `/feedbacks`.
- [ ] Para rodar localmente contra o backend local, criar `frontend/.env.local` com `VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:5000/api` e reiniciar o `npm run dev`.
- [ ] Conferir que o login do
items.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]

items.title: feat(council): anthropic_trinity_v1 n8n — Opus Director + parallel Opus/Sonnet/Haiku + Opus Synthesis
items.content:
## Summary

New n8n workflow `council/anthropic_trinity_v1.n8n.json` that links all three Anthropic family members — Opus, Sonnet, Haiku — into a single loop so they can work together, with Opus acting as director and synthesizer.

**Why this file didn't exist already:** the only prior n8n was `council_of_seven_v1.n8n.json`, which wires a single Anthropic seat (Seat 2) hardcoded to one Opus variant. There was no workflow covering the three family members as distinct nodes, and no future-proof way to pick up new Claude releases.

## Flow

```
Manual Trigger
  → Set Task (task + registry_url)
  → Registry Refresh      (HTTP GET COUNCIL_MODEL_REGISTRY.json)
  → Resolve Models        (pick opus / sonnet / haiku IDs from seat 2 alternatives)
  → Director_Opus         (decomposes task into 3 role-matched sub-prompts)
  → Parse Director Plan   (JSON parse with graceful fallback)
  → parallel:
      ├── Member_Opus     (depth, judgment)
      ├── Member_Sonnet   (balanced general-purpose)
      └── Member_Haiku    (fast, concise)
  → Merge Responses
  → Build Synthesis Prompt
  → Synthesis_Opus
  → Trinity Output
```

## Registry-driven — future releases auto-wire

`Registry Refresh` fetches the canonical registry over HTTP; `Resolve Models` walks `seats[seat_id==2].alternatives` and picks the current `claude-opus-*`, `claude-sonnet-*`, `claude-haiku-*` by rank. Bumping a model version in `COUNCIL_MODEL_REGISTRY.json` is enough — no workflow edit required. Hardcoded defaults only kick in if the registry fetch fails.

## Robustness

- All 5 Anthropic HTTP nodes: `continueOnFail: true`.
- Director output is parsed with fence-stripping and brace-rebalancing; if it's not clean JSON, a uniform fallback plan is used.
- Synthesis proceeds from whatever subset of members responded (mirrors Gabriel's behaviour in `council_of_seven_v1`).

## Why a "TrinityClaw-shaped" workflow, not a new claw module

Proposed on the thread: a new OpenClaw-class module (`AnthropiClaw` / `TrinityClaw`)

items.title: [Feedback] Rework Wolverine so it gets 5 attacks per ability
items.content:
### Before submitting

- [x] I searched open and closed issues and this is not a duplicate
- [x] This report covers one suggestion or balance concern

### Type

Suggestion (new feature or mechanic)

### Animal / Feature

Wolverine

### What feels wrong or what's missing?

Wolverine as an animal has been bland for the longest time. Its ability is just Lion and Hippo's, but on an animal which isn't as fitting for the concept. I think Wolverine should be an animal that can apply constant pressure if used well, so I want its ability to play into that. I understand that a full rework is maybe a bit much, but given the schedule for the rewrite's updates, I want to pitch this now anyways, since I do think Wolverine needs a rework the most out of any animal now.

### What should change?

- Wolverine's ability can now be used 5 times before it goes on cooldown. The first 4 ability uses are slashes, and the final use is a chomp.
- The slashes do 0.3 bites of damage to prey, 0.1 bites to predators, and 0.5 seconds of stun to both.
- The chomp does 0.6 bites of damage to prey, 0.3 bites to predators, and 1.5 seconds of stun to both.
- There is a 250ms delay between when Wolverine can use its ability again, but each slash and the chomp also last 250ms.
- Once the chomp is used, the ability goes on cooldown. If possible, the cooldown should vary from 8-16 seconds, depending on how much time passes between the initial ability use and final use (for every 5 seconds between initial and final use, the cooldown becomes 1 second shorter, capping out at a minimum of an 8 second cooldown).
- If the above point isn't possible, the cooldown should be 12-13 seconds long.
- Wolverine's ability icon should also show how many uses it has before it goes on cooldown (example in the image below).

### Why would this help?

With multiple uses of its ability before going on cooldown, Wolverine would become way more flexible. It would be able to mix up its ability uses in a way that encourages smart

items.title: feat(claw): autonomy hardening — sanitizer, async exec, launchd
items.content:
## Summary
Cierra los gaps críticos de autonomía identificados en el audit de Dr. Strange (Claw). ~70% de la infraestructura ya existía; este PR cableó los 3 items restantes de mayor impacto.

- **P1 — Sanitizer PostToolUse hook**: flag `ingests_external_content` en `ToolDefinition`, helper `sanitize_tool_output()` que ignora patrones dentro de code fences (mitiga falsos positivos) y devuelve quarantine visible cuando detecta injection. Activado en `WebSearch`, `WebFetch`, `Firecrawl{Scrape,Search,Extract}`.
- **P2 — Async tool execution**: `ToolRegistry.execute_async()` delega a `asyncio.to_thread(self.execute)` para que shell/HTTP/file I/O no bloqueen el event loop. Call sites sync existentes se mantienen (cambio quirúrgico).
- **P3 — launchd installer**: `scripts/install_launchd.sh` idempotente con `plutil -lint`, validación de rutas absolutas (python, launcher) y `launchctl bootstrap`/`kickstart`. El plist `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pachano.claw.plist` ya existía; ahora es reinstalable de forma reproducible.

P4 (verificación post-acción formal) y P5 (FSM) quedan fuera de alcance — `RetryStuckPolicy` ya mitiga la mayoría de bucles problemáticos.

## Test plan
- [x] `pytest tests/test_tools.py -v` — 12/12 passed (5 nuevos: malicious detection, code-fence regression, legit content passthrough, flag-off no-op, async parity)
- [x] Suite completa: 668 passed, 18 failed pre-existentes sin cambio (baseline: 663 passed)
- [x] `plutil -lint` sobre el plist template
- [x] Supervisor cargado: `launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/com.pachano.claw` reporta `state = running`
- [ ] QA manual en Telegram: pedir a Claw una búsqueda web y confirmar que el output llega con prefijo `[EXTERNAL:...]`
- [ ] QA manual: inyectar contenido con `ignore previous instructions` en un fixture de WebFetch y confirmar quarantine en `ObserveStream`
- [ ] QA manual: `kill -9 <pid>` y confirmar reinicio automático <10s desde checkpoint

items.title: [agento] fix: metadata-updater hook remove overbroad shell operator blocking
items.content:
## Summary
- Remove overbroad shell operator blocklist from metadata-updater.sh PreToolUse hook tokenizer
- The tokenizer was blocking legitimate piped commands (ps aux | grep, launchctl 2>&1 | head)
- Dangerous command chaining (gh pr create/merge, git checkout -b) already caught by is_guarded_segment() checks

## Test plan
- [x] echo test 2>&1 now passes (was blocked by & in blocklist)
- [x] ps aux | grep -v grep | head -5 passes (was blocked by | in blocklist)
- [x] launchctl print 2>&1 | head passes (was blocked)
- [x] Guarded commands still blocked: echo x && gh pr merge correctly denied

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> This relaxes command parsing/guard logic in a security-sensitive PreToolUse hook, potentially changing which shell command forms are treated as safe vs denied. Because policy enforcement depends on correct tokenization, allowing previously-blocked operators could introduce bypasses for guarded commands if not covered by downstream checks.
> 
> **Overview**
> Removes the tokenizer’s hard blocklist of shell operator characters in `.claude/metadata-updater.sh`, so commands containing operators like pipes/redirections are no longer rejected up front.
> 
> The hook now relies on the later `is_guarded_segment()`/chaining analysis to decide when to deny unsafe command sequences, expanding the set of shell syntax that can pass through the PreToolUse normalization step.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 7803c088c7ec6afc85fdc9ba24abe409721dc051. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
  * Updated command tokenization logic to defer validation of shell operators to downstream safety checks instead of immediate rejection during parsing.

items.title: Batch 5 — P0 Errors & Bugs: RC-A retrieval fix + plugin bundle-dist refactor + recent_facts GDPR scrub
items.content:
## Summary

Batch 5 of the P0 testing plan — 5 error/bug flows (TF-ER-001/002/003/004/009). During live verification of TD-10 (successful_use tracking), a CRITICAL bug was discovered: **auto-recall via the scored working set has never worked in OSS production**. RC-A — retrieval query was the host gateway's prompt envelope instead of the user's actual question. This PR:

1. **Fixes RC-A** (TD-54) via plugin-side envelope stripping
2. **Refactors both TS plugins** to a unified bundle-dist layout (aligned with community default plugin `lossless-claw`) to resolve long-standing dual-source drift that masked the fix
3. **Resolves GDPR re-creation loop** (Phase 4 TD #2) — `MemoryStoreFacade.delete()` now scrubs the `recent_facts` Redis buffer

Unit tests: 2,532/0 (+3 from baseline 2,529). Integration: 156/0. TypeScript plugins: 48/0 + 32/0. Zero regressions.

| `a4c534d` | fix(retrieval): forward OpenClaw params.prompt as retrieval query (TD-54 RC-A v1) |

items.title: v2.6.0-rc.1: verifiable RC — provenance workflow, backend hardening, schemas, dashboard & CI
items.content:
### Motivation

- Prepare a verifiable v2.6 release candidate with provenance artifacts, deterministic manifests, and operator guidance. 
- Harden backend indexing and governance read-models to be idempotent and reorg‑safe and expose proof/governance surfaces. 
- Add canonical v2.6 JSON schemas and SDK types for threshold/decryption contract surfaces to enable automated validation. 

### Description

- CI and release: add `.github/workflows/ci.yml` for tests/typechecks and `.github/workflows/release-provenance.yml` to build a source archive, generate a deterministic provenance manifest, create `SHA256SUMS`, generate an SBOM, and attest/upload artifacts. 
- Backend hardening: add migration `backend/migrations/002_v26_hardening.sql`, indexer improvements in `backend/app/indexer.py` for multi-contract sources, reorg rewind deletes of derived rows, confirmation/safe-tip cursor, ordered causal processing, governance view upserts, and a deterministic backfill runner `backend/app/backfill.py`. 
- API & schemas: expand `backend/app/main.py` with readiness, proof, governance and metrics endpoints and OpenAPI export, add Pydantic models in `backend/app/schemas.py`, and update `backend/requirements.txt`. 
- Contracts & ABIs: surface `RELEASE_VERSION`/`releaseMetadata` in `contracts/NovaSeedRegistryV25.sol` and ship ABI snapshots under `contracts/abi/` plus an export helper `scripts/contracts/export_abi.py`. 
- Release tooling & docs: add `scripts/release/generate_provenance_manifest.py`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `RELEASES.md`, contributor/support/security docs, proof docket templates under `docs/`, green-path/runbook, and verification guide `docs/verify-release.md`. 
- Schemas & examples: add canonical schemas and examples under `schemas/v2.6/` for `decryption-attestation` and `threshold-binding-profile`. 
- Dashboard & SDK: refresh dashboard (`dashboard/*`) to surface proof/governance/provenance and add SDK types `sdk/shared/types.ts` and lockfile for reproducible SDK installs. 
- Tes

items.title: feat(providers): claude-code-acp — run Hermes on a Claude subscription, plus ACP timeout fixes
items.content:
## TL;DR

Adds a new inference provider, **`claude-code-acp`**, that runs Hermes's reasoning loop inside the Anthropic Claude Code CLI via the [`@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp) adapter — billed against your Claude Pro / Max subscription, not a metered `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. The full Hermes experience (`SOUL.md` persona, skills, memory, the 90+ tool registry, hooks, auto-skill-creation) is injected into the subprocess through a per-session sandbox + MCP sidecars, so from the user's seat nothing feels different — except the bill.

Also in this branch: a surgical three-part fix for the 2026-04-18 Claudalyst ACP-timeout incident, **and** a follow-up cross-platform fix for the `edit_message(finalize=...)` contract that was breaking streaming edits on every non-DingTalk gateway.

---

## Why

Hermes already supports ~10 hosted providers plus local endpoints. Two gaps remained:

1. **No way to use a Claude subscription.** Users on Pro/Max pay a flat monthly fee for Claude Code and would rather spend that quota than stack a metered API bill on top.
2. **ACP transport was copy-pasted.** The existing `copilot-acp` client carried its own JSON-RPC framing, session dispatch, and fs shims. Adding a second ACP provider meant either forking all of that or extracting it — extraction won.

## Architecture

### The sandbox pattern

Every ACP session gets a per-session directory at `~/.hermes/runtime/claude-code/<session_id>/` containing everything the subprocess needs to *be* Hermes:

```
<sandbox>/
├── CLAUDE.md                         # SOUL.md + memory snapshot + toolbelt hint
├── .claude/
│   ├── skills/<skill>/SKILL.md       # flattened Hermes skills
│   └── settings.local.json           # pins permissions.defaultMode + model
├── .mcp.json                         # points at hermes mcp tools-serve + hermes mcp serve
└── .hermes-sandbox.json              # manifest hash for cache invalidation
```

The subprocess is s

items.title: fix(openai-completions): enable streaming usage for vLLM + local OpenAI-compat servers
items.content:
## Summary

- **Problem:** On `api: "openai-completions"`, providers served from local or custom endpoints — vLLM (reported), LocalAI, SGLang, llama.cpp `--server`, Jan, LM Studio, text-generation-webui, TabbyAPI — have `supportsUsageInStreaming: false`. The transport never sends `stream_options: { include_usage: true }`, streams return no usage (per the OpenAI Chat Completions spec), and `openclaw status` renders `unknown/131k (?%)` for every affected session. Context pruning, `lossless-claw` compaction decisions, and the subagent lifecycle all silently degrade.
- **Why it matters:** Local OpenAI-compatible servers are the primary self-hosted path recommended in the docs, and vLLM is the most common of them. This turns the token-accounting UI into dead weight for users on that path.
- **What changed:** Whitelist extension in `resolveOpenAICompletionsCompatDefaults` to include known OpenAI-spec-compliant local inference servers alongside Ollama. Users running these servers under custom provider names can still opt in via the pre-existing request-time override at `openai-transport-stream.ts:1288-1289` (`compat.supportsUsageInStreaming ?? detected.supportsUsageInStreaming`).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to the transport stream, the usage accumulator, `persistSessionUsageUpdate`, the context engine, `clearStaleAssistantUsageOnSessionMessages`, or the status display. The existing "does not broaden streaming usage for generic custom providers" invariant (in `openai-completions-compat.test.ts`) is preserved verbatim — only *named* known-good providers and *explicit* user overrides take the new path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (provider compat layer)
- [ ] API / contracts
items.author: gaineyllc

items.title: [Feature] 活动 Post 自动化发布流程（OpenClaw Agent + Telegram）
items.content:
## 背景

每次活动结束后，手动撰写并发布活动 recap post（含图片、视频、骑行数据）流程繁琐。目标是通过 OpenClaw（龙虾）AI Agent + Telegram 将整个流程自动化，实现"口述 → 生成 Markdown → 一键发布"。

---

## 完整工作流

```
[活动结束]
    │
    ▼
用户在 Telegram 向 ACC Post Agent 口述活动概况
（参与人数、骑行里程、活动亮点等）
    │
    ├── 发送精选照片（多张）
    │
    ├── 指定对应的网站活动页面（事件报名 URL / event slug）
    │
    ▼
Agent 自动拉取该活动在 ACC 网站的元数据
（标题、日期、路线、报名信息等）
    │
    ▼
Agent 生成 Markdown 草稿
（图片按合适位置插入，文字结合口述内容 + 活动元数据）
    │
    ├── [若涉及视频]
    │       │
    │       ▼
    │   Agent 提醒用户上传视频至 YouTube / 哔哩哔哩
    │   用户回传视频链接
    │   Agent 将视频嵌入 Markdown
    │
    ▼
用户审阅 Markdown 草稿
    │
    ├── 不满意 → 用户给出修改意见 → Agent 修订
    │
    └── 满意 → 确认发布
              │
              ▼
         Agent 自动将 Post 发布到 ACC 网站
         （无需手动操作 GitHub）
```

---

## 技术实现要点

### 1. OpenClaw Skill 设计

为 ACC Post Agent 设计一个专用 Skill，包含以下能力：

- **内容采集**：接收口述文字 + 照片 + （可选）视频链接
- **元数据拉取**：根据 event slug 调用 ACC 后端 API 获取活动详情
- **Markdown 生成**：按照 ACC Post 模板格式撰写，图片/视频插入合适位置
- **交互修订**：支持多轮对话修改草稿
- **发布执行**：确认后调用 ACC 后端 API 发布 Post（写入内容仓库或数据库）

### 2. ACC 后端需新增的 API

| 接口 | 用途 |
|------|------|
| `GET /api/events/{slug}` | 拉取活动元数据供 Agent 使用 |
| `POST /api/posts` | 接收 Markdown 内容，发布为活动 recap post |
| `POST /api/media/upload` | （可选）Agent 代理上传图片至 CDN |

> ⚠️ 发布 API 需要鉴权（仅 Right Leader / Admin 可调用）

### 3. Telegram Bot 集成

- ACC Post Agent 通过 Telegram Bot 与用户交互
- 支持接收图片（多张）和文字消息
- 支持多轮对话（修改草稿）

### 4. 前端 Post 展示

- 新增 `posts/` 内容区（或复用现有 blog 结构）
- 支持 Markdown 渲染，含图片、YouTube / 哔哩哔哩视频嵌入

---

## 待讨论问题

- [ ] Post 存储方式：Markdown 文件提交到 GitHub repo？还是存入数据库？（影响发布 API 设计）
- [ ] 图片托管：上传到哪个 CDN / 对象存储？（Cloudflare R2 / Supabase Storage / GitHub 仓库？）
- [ ] 视频嵌入格式：YouTube iframe？还是仅放超链接？
- [ ] OpenClaw Skill 触发词 / 对话入口如何设计？
- [ ] 发布鉴权：Telegram Bot 如何验证操作者身份？

---

## 验收标准

- [ ] Telegram 向 Agent 口述活动信息 + 发图，能获得完整 Markdown 草稿
- [ ] 草稿含图片（正确位置）、活动元数据、骑行数据
- [ ] 涉及视频时，Agent 主动提示并支持嵌入视频链接
- [ ] 用户确认后，一条指令完成发布，无需手动操作 GitHub
- [ ] 发布后 ACC 网站可正常显示该 Post（含图片、视频）
- [ ] 全流程有鉴权保护，防止非授权发布

---

## 相关模块

- `backend/` — 新增 Posts API、Ev

items.title: PyO3 CryptoPerpetual.from_dict fails on Chinese base assets
items.content:
# Bug Report

## Confirmation

**Before opening a bug report, please confirm:**

- [x] I’ve re-read the relevant sections of the documentation.
- [x] I’ve searched existing issues and discussions to avoid duplicates.
- [x] I’ve reviewed or skimmed the source code (or examples) to confirm the behavior is not by design.
- [x] I’ve tested this issue using a recent *development* wheel (`dev` develop or `a` nightly) and can still reproduce it.

Checking a recent development wheel can save time because the issue may already have been fixed.
You can install a development wheel by running:

```bash
pip install -U nautilus_trader --pre --index-url https://packages.nautechsystems.io/simple
```

See the [development-wheels](https://github.com/nautechsystems/nautilus_trader#development-wheels) section for more details.

### Expected Behavior

`CryptoPerpetual.from_dict(raw_instrument)` creates a CryptoPerpetual instrument.

### Actual Behavior

`CryptoPerpetual.from_dict(raw_instrument) throws a ValueError`

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem

Run the following snippet in an interactive Python session:

```Python
from nautilus_trader.model.instruments import CryptoPerpetual as PythonCryptoPerpetual
from nautilus_trader.core.nautilus_pyo3 import CryptoPerpetual as RustCryptoPerpetual

raw_instrument = {
    "base_currency": "\u9f99\u867e",

items.title: 苏中浪游
items.content:
基于对（龙年）春节期间住宿条件、潜在冲突等各种因素的评估，我早早决定过了年初一就出去浪一圈。以旅游为目的的独自远行，有生以来还是第一次，可见之前的人生，有多么平淡庸常缺乏自我。当时两湖和安徽在下冻雨，开远路不免对莫测天气有些顾虑，假日后期又有同学聚会要赶回，于是就规划了一条周边的路线。

初二一早驱车直奔泰州，之前对泰州的了解好像仅限于[泰州学派](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%B3%B0%E5%B7%9E%E5%AD%A6%E6%B4%BE)，王艮、李贽等思想家。到了才知道，泰州有2100多年的建城史，古称海阳、海陵，自古有“**水陆要津，咽喉据郡**”之谓，施耐庵、郑板桥、柳敬亭、丁文江、梅兰芳等都是泰州籍人士，而我，起初动念赴泰州主要是冲着品尝泰州早茶去的😆。

正午时分到达酒店，草草吃过点心，就去参观扬郡试院，即扬州府属八县童生考秀才的试场。从康熙至光绪朝，试院一直由省学政主持考试，所以又称为学政试院。《海陵竹枝词》有诗纪述：_争来试院考名流，八邑英才集此州。爆响十声香案发，几家欢乐几家愁_。

出来一看天色还早，又去乔园、柳园打卡。乔园始建于明万历年间，是苏北地区现存最古老的园林之一，咸丰朝为两淮盐运使乔松年购得，遂称“乔园”。园内亭榭参差，清流映带，茂林深竹，景象幽奇。如陈从周言，“_在意境上以幽雅清淡着眼，如芭蕉分绿，疏筠横窗，天竹腊梅，苍松古柏，交枝成图，相掩生趣_”。

乔园里面楹联颇多。如某介绍泰州古代盐税文化的展厅，有联曰“_帆通里河米，市换过江盐_”。里河，即里下河，里下河地区涉及江苏省扬州、泰州、南通、淮安、盐城等城市。程越华[此文](https://renrendoc.com/paper/175839929.html)对乔园楹联介绍甚详。

乔园出来，又赴柳园走马观花。拟收一本《柳敬亭研究》来读。“_既在军中久，其豪猾大侠、杀人亡命，流离遇合、破家失国之事，无不身亲见之；且五方土音、乡俗好尚，习见习闻。每发一声，使人闻之，或如刀剑铁骑，飒然浮空；或如风号雨泣，鸟悲兽骇。亡国之恨顿生，檀板之声无色，有非莫生之言可尽者矣。_”

回住处略作休整，晚上去逛桃园老街，其实也是07年才兴建的仿古建筑群。凤城河上楼船箫鼓，声光相乱；老街火树银花，熙来攘往，处处欢声笑语，好不热闹。还看到了无人机摆出锦鳞游泳、望海楼名胜等造型。不禁感叹，“来泰州，遇见尘世幸福”，信然。

住处离会宾楼很近，惟生意火爆，须六点半到场拿号，去晚了就要等位了，怕耽误后续溱湖湿地公园的行程，我定好闹钟，次日曙光未露就骑了哈罗电动车（投放量很大，短途出行很方便）到店，点了烫干丝、鱼汤馄饨、蟹黄包、虾仁蒸饺，吃得肚皮滚圆，心满意足往姜堰去也。

事先买的溱湖湿地公园+溱潼古镇的联票，外地游客享受新春优惠价70元，对泰州三区的居民不收门票。赞赏之余不由得要吐槽下南通，狼山一个小土包，也要收70元，对南通市民竟无一分一厘优惠，钻钱眼里了。下船进入景区第一站是湿地科普馆，适合年轻父母们带着小朋友来增长知识。

因春气初动，百花待放，新绿未萌，入眼惟见蒹葭苍茫，渚云烟树，景色比初夏或深秋时是要差一些的。只能想象下彼时芰荷风起、凫雁杂集，“_舟横春水渡，人醉落花村。胜游应不倦，归院欲黄昏_。”“_水滟接横塘，花多碍舟路。绿汀渔网集，隔浦菱歌度_。”的景致和意境了。

此后又去了溱潼古镇、黄桥古镇，鱼饼虾球、黄桥烧饼，一路逛吃逛吃，其间也很庄敬地瞻仰了新四军黄桥战役纪念馆和中国地质学开拓者、“宁死不吃中药不看中医”的丁文江先生的故居。初五一早到如皋，吃过四海楼的蟹黄包早餐，逛了冒辟疆董小宛幽栖的水绘园，忽动归念，于是退订客房，直奔南通狼山风景区。

狼山是“全国佛教八小名山”之首，又称“江海第一山”，身为南通人，至今未登临过，痛骂武瞾“_入门见嫉，蛾眉不肯让人；掩袖工谗，狐媚偏能惑主_”、霸气宣称“_试看今日之域中，竟是谁家之天下_”的骆宾王的墓也未曾瞻仰，所以短途游的最后一站定在狼山。

适逢初五“迎财神”，天气晴暖，鸟语花香，杜诗“_迟日江山丽，春风花草香_”正合此景。各郡生民纷至沓来进香祈福，把山脚围得水泄不通，只能把车停得远远的步行到检票口。决定快上快下，只在望江亭、大观台稍作停留，风卷潮声入耳，云连海气吹面，感受罢大江东去、日边帆影的磅礴气象便匆匆下山，寻路东归。
items.author: jslilei

items.title: fix(vision): 改用 claude -p subprocess 走订阅账号，不再直连 Anthropic API
items.content:
## Summary

- `image_vision_review.py` 从直连 Anthropic REST API 改为 subprocess 调 `claude -p` CLI
- 走本地 Claude Max 订阅（`CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=~/.claude-accountX`），零 API 扣费
- 原因：Anthropic API 账号余额不足，pipeline Stage 5 vision 审查 100% 挂在 credit_too_low → 全部 skipped

## Changes

- 删除 base64/urllib 直调 Anthropic messages API 的逻辑（`_load_anthropic_api_key` / `_encode_image` 等）
- 新增 subprocess 调 `claude -p` CLI；图片路径内嵌在 prompt 里，由 Claude 自己的 Read 工具读（claude CLI 2.1.x 不支持 `--image` 参数）
- env：`CLAUDE_CLI_PATH` / `IMAGE_VISION_TIMEOUT`（默认 90s） / `VISION_CLAUDE_ACCOUNT`（默认 `account2`，避免与父对话 account1 的 CLAUDE.md 上下文污染）
- 保留 `review_images()` 签名 + 返回格式不变；保持失败策略：vision 调用失败 → skipped，不阻塞 pipeline

## Test plan

- [x] 17 个单测全过（mock `subprocess.run`；断言 `-p` / `--dangerously-skip-permissions` / prompt 含图路径 / `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` 正确 / `CLAUDECODE` 被移除）
- [x] Pipeline 端到端：对真实 cover.png 跑 `review_images()`，8.7s 返回 major severity（主动检出底部文字裁切和右侧贴边问题）

## 真实验证输出

```
$ IMAGE_VISION_TIMEOUT=180 VISION_CLAUDE_ACCOUNT=account1 python3 -c "
from pipeline_worker.image_vision_review import review_images
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('.../cards/为什么-2026-年龙虾-让一人公司成为为了可能-cover.png')
r = review_images([p])
print(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
"

INFO pipeline-worker.image_vision_review [vision] 完成: checked=1 skipped=0 severity=major pass=False
elapsed: 8.7 s
{
  "review_passed": false,
  "checked": 1,
  "skipped": 0,
  "issues": [
    "cover.png: 底部 ZenithJoy 后面的文字被裁切/重叠，'完整花切5' 类字符显示异常",
    "cover.png: 右侧竖排'大湖成长日记(AI+)'贴近右边缘，底部括号文字几乎贴边"
  ],
  "severity": "major"
}
```

对比老版本（直连 Anthropic API）：全部返回 `skipped` + 日志 `HTTP 400 credit balance is too low`。

items.title: [Bug]: Feishu channel: Local image files sent as path text instead of uploaded image
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

When using the `message` tool with `media` parameter to send a local image file to Feishu channel, the bot sends the file path as plain text (e.g., `/home/user/image.png`) instead of uploading the image to Feishu and displaying it as an inline image.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use the message tool to send a local image:
   message(action=send, channel=feishu, media="/path/to/image.png")
2. The message received in Feishu shows the file path as text, not an image

### Expected behavior

Local image files should be automatically uploaded to Feishu servers via the `/im/v1/images` API, then sent as an image message with `image_key` so it displays inline in the chat.

### Actual behavior

When using the message tool with media parameter to send a local image file, the Feishu bot sends the file path as plain text (e.g., "/home/user/image.png") instead of displaying the image inline.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.9 (0512059)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

npm

### Model

minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7

### Provider / routing chain

minimax-portal

### Additional provider/model setup details

N/A

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Medium - Local image files cannot be displayed inline in Feishu chat, only file path text is shown. This is a common use case for sharing images.

### Additional information

Workaround available: Use Feishu API directly to upload image and get image_key, then send message with image_key. Script available at /home/chenxr/OpenClaw/scripts/feishu-send-image.py
This discovery comes from my OpenClaw - Lobster Mars （大龙虾玛尔斯）

items.title: fix(pipeline): person_data 补 timeline/schedule/qa + image_review 拒绝占位符
items.content:
## 背景
主理人打开生成的图发现 "待补充/暂无数据" 占位文字满屏。根因：
- `person_data_builder` prompt 只让 LLM 生成 `name/handle/headline/key_stats/flywheel/quote` 六字段
- `timeline/day_schedule/qa` 走 `gen-v6-person.mjs` 模板内置兜底（`{year:'-', title:'待补充', desc:'暂无数据'}`）
- `_build_fallback` 里也填 `"待补充"`（不用 findings 真内容）
- `image_review` 没有对 `person-data.json` 做占位符硬规则

## 变更
### 1. `services/creator/pipeline_worker/person_data_builder.py`
- 扩 prompt 要求 LLM 输出 `timeline(5) / day_schedule(4) / qa(4)`，含字符预算
- `BUDGET` 补 `timeline_year/title/desc`、`schedule_time/title/desc`、`qa_q/a`
- `_enforce_budget` 数组长度不足时用 findings 真实 `title/content` 补齐（阶段标签、早/中/晚时段、默认提问）——**永不写占位符**
- `_clean_placeholder` 把 LLM 偷懒返回的 `"待补充/暂无数据/待产出/-"` 清洗为真实内容
- 最终防御：`_build_fallback`（LLM 完全不可达）也用 findings 真字段填

### 2. `services/creator/pipeline_worker/executors/image_review.py`
- 新增 `_check_person_data_placeholders(out_dir)`：读 `cards/person-data.json`，命中 `待补充/暂无数据/待产出` 或 `key_stats.val == "-"` / `timeline.year == "-"` 都上报
- 发现占位符 → `review_passed=False` + `vision_severity=major` + `issues=["person-data 含占位符，LLM 生成不完整"]`

### 3. 单测
- `test_person_data_builder.py`：
  - 扩 `test_llm_happy_path`：断言 timeline/schedule/qa 长度 5/4/4 + 字段预算 + 无占位符
  - 新增 `test_llm_missing_timeline_schedule_qa_fields`：LLM 缺字段，parser 用 findings 真内容补齐
  - 新增 `test_llm_returns_placeholder_text_gets_cleaned`：LLM 偷懒回 `"待补充"` 被清洗
  - 补 `test_llm_invalid_json_fallback / test_llm_unreachable_fallback / test_empty_findings_fallback`：断言无占位符
  - `test_key_stats_padding` 改成断言"不等于待补充"（用 findings 填）
- `test_image_review_with_vision.py`：新增 `TestImageReviewPersonDataPlaceholder`（含占位符 FAIL + 干净 pass 两用例）

## 验证
- 45 个 pipeline_worker 单测全绿
- 114 个 creator 全量单测全绿
- 本机实调 Cecelia LLM（thalamus tier）生成完整 person-data，所有字段齐全、grep 无占位符

## 验收路径（主 session 会跑）
1. 删现有龙虾 pipeline 的 person-data.json
2. `build_person_data('为什么 2026 年龙虾 让一人公司成为为了可能', findings)`
3. 跑 `gen-v6-person.mjs` → 9 张 PNG
4. grep 新生成 PNG 不应出现 "待补充/暂无数据"
5. 5 张时间点、4 张 day_schedule、4 张 qa 都有真内容

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](h

items.title: feat(pipeline): 图片质检 A+B — LLM person-data 合规化 + vision 图片审查
items.content:
## 背景（证据看坏图）
Pipeline 8d4682d6 跑完 9 张 PNG 全部爆边界：
- **cover.png**：头像圈写"为什"（keyword 前 2 字被模板裁切）
- **key_stats 底部**："在2026年，随着智能体AI（Ag" 文字溢出
- 坏图路径：`/Users/administrator/content-output/2026-04-18-为什么-2026-年龙虾-让一人公司成为为了可能/cards/*.png`

## 根因
- **层 A**：`generate.py` 硬塞 `name = keyword` 整段、`key_stats[].label = finding.title[:20]`
- **层 B**：`image_review.py` 只查文件存在 + 数量上限，不看图内容 → 坏图静默通过

## 解决
### 层 A — `person_data_builder.py`（新文件）
LLM（Cecelia /api/brain/llm-service/generate, tier=cortex）按 V6 模板严格预算生成 `person-data.json`：

| 字段 | 预算 |
|---|---|
| name | ≤ 8 字 |
| handle | ≤ 18 字 |
| headline | ≤ 20 字 |
| key_stats[].val / label / sub | ≤ 8 / 12 / 15 字 |
| flywheel[] | ≤ 6 字 |
| flywheel_insight | ≤ 28 字 |
| quote | ≤ 36 字 |

- `_enforce_budget` 防御性硬截断（LLM 越权也收敛）
- LLM 不可达 fallback：`_fallback_name` 启发式抽短名（"为什么 2026 年龙虾..." → "龙虾"）

### 层 B — `image_vision_review.py`（新文件）
Claude vision（`~/.credentials/anthropic.json` 同 brain）扫描 9 张 PNG：
- 检测文字溢出 / 布局重叠 / 字体异常 / 整体视觉
- severity=major → `review_passed=false`
- severity=minor → pass 但上报
- API 挂掉 / 无 key → skipped + pass（保守不阻塞 pipeline）

### 改造
- `executors/generate.py`：调 `build_person_data` 替代硬塞 keyword
- `executors/image_review.py`：集成 `review_images`、从 `out_dir/cards/*.png` 读图、vision major 强制 FAIL、`SKIP_VISION_REVIEW=1` 可跳过

## 测试（25 个新用例）
- `test_person_data_builder.py`：happy path / markdown fence / 超预算 / 非法 JSON / 不可达 / 空 findings / fallback 启发式
- `test_image_vision_review.py`：无图 / 全 major / 全 ok / mix severity / vision 失败保守跳过 / 无 key
- `test_image_review_with_vision.py`：major 实锤 FAIL / ok 放行 / SKIP 短路 / 异常不阻塞

110 个 creator 测试全绿。

## 手工验证（本地跑过）
### 旧版硬塞 vs 新版 fallback（同一 keyword + findings）
- 旧：`name="为什么 2026 年龙虾 让一人公司成为为了可能"` + `label="在2026年，随着智能体AI（Agent"`
- 新 fallback：`name="龙虾"` + `label="在2026年，随着智能体"` (≤12 字)
- 新 LLM 理想输出：`name="龙虾效应"` + `flywheel=["洞察","产品","分发","反哺"]`

### Vision 对老坏图的判断
mock vision 响应下，9 张坏图 6 张判 major、3 张 minor，`review_passed=false`，实锤拦截。
真实 API 调用本地返回 HTTP 400 credit balance too low（主理人需充值 Anth

items.title: Translate documentation to Chinese
items.content:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Why it matters:
- What changed:
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause:
- Missing detection / guardrail:
- Contributing context (if known):

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
- Scenario the test should lock in:
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
- If no new test is added, why not:

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A

items.author: Tyaoo

items.title: Slack: internal replyToId can override valid thread anchor and leak replies to channel root
items.content:
## Summary

Slack replies can leak to the channel root when `[[reply_to_current]]` resolves to an internal OpenClaw message id instead of a real Slack `thread_ts`, and the Slack outbound path gives that `replyToId` priority over the known thread anchor.

This appears in inter-session / subagent-completion flows where the current message is an internal event, not the original Slack message.

## User-visible symptom

A reply that should stay inside an existing Slack thread is posted as a new top-level channel message.

## Reproduction shape

This is intermittent, but the failure pattern is consistent:

1. A Slack thread is active and the agent session is correctly thread-bound.
2. A resumed turn is triggered by an internal event, for example:
   - subagent completion
   - inter-session completion/update event
   - another internal message delivered into the agent session
3. The assistant emits `[[reply_to_current]]` in the final reply.
4. The current message for that turn is now an internal OpenClaw message, so the resolved `replyToId` is an internal message id, not a Slack ts like `1776546991.037129`.
5. Slack outbound/reply transport uses `replyToId` before the known `threadId` / `replyThreadTs`.
6. The resulting Slack send loses the real thread anchor and posts to channel root.

## Why this is distinct from older Slack thread_ts bugs

Earlier Slack issues focused on:
- missing `thread_ts` in delivery context
- DM thread persistence
- routed reply normalization gaps
- inter-agent messages surfacing visibly

This issue is a separate guardrail failure:

> a non-Slack internal reply id is allowed to override a valid Slack thread anchor.

Even if thread context is otherwise present, the wrong priority rule can still break delivery.

## Root cause hypothesis

Slack-specific outbound logic accepts `payload.replyToId` too broadly.

In the failing path, `replyToId` is an internal OpenClaw message id, not a Slack ts. If that value is used as the effective reply reference bef

items.title: [Bug]: Windows: spawn claude ENOENT "claude" missing from cmdCommands shim allowlist
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

On Windows, OpenClaw fails to spawn the Claude CLI with `Error: spawn claude ENOENT`. Every model in the fallback chain fails. Root cause: `"claude"` is missing from the `cmdCommands` allowlist used by `resolveWindowsCommandShim`, so `.cmd` is never appended and Node's `spawn()` can't resolve `claude.cmd` on Windows.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Official install script: `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`
2. Install the Claude CLI (Anthropic's `claude.cmd` shim on PATH).
3. Start OpenClaw and send any message that routes to `provider=claude-cli`.

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw resolves `claude` to `claude.cmd` on Windows (same way it already does for `npm`, `pnpm`, `yarn`, `npx`) and the CLI backend process starts successfully.
Root cause is in `src/process/supervisor/adapters/child.ts`:
`resolveCommand` calls `resolveWindowsCommandShim({ command, cmdCommands: ["npm","pnpm","yarn","npx"] })` `"claude"` is not in the list, so the shim returns `"claude"` unchanged. Node `spawn()` on Windows can't find `.cmd` shims without `shell: true` or an explicit extension → ENOENT.

Fix: add `"claude"` to that `cmdCommands` array

### Actual behavior

Spawn fails immediately for every model in the fallback chain:

  [agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=opus promptChars=984
  [process/supervisor] spawn failed: reason=Error: spawn claude ENOENT
  [model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7 ... detail=spawn claude
  ENOENT
  ... (repeats for sonnet-4-6, opus-4-6, opus-4-5, sonnet-4-5, haiku-4-5) ...
  Embedded agent failed before reply: spawn claude ENOENT

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.15

### Operating system

Windows 11 Pro (build 10.0.26200)

### Install method

curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash

### Model

claude-opus-4.7

### Provider / routing chain

provider=claude-cli

###

items.title: feat: add openclaw doctor dry-run and remote repair workflow
items.content:
## What changed
- add the bundled `openclaw-doctor` skill and readonly launcher for sandbox-safe triage
- add first-class `openclaw doctor --dry-run` support with explicit inspect/dry-run/apply mode routing and preview output across repair paths
- add a dedicated `doctor.run` gateway RPC plus `openclaw_doctor_repair` agent tool for remote-only preview/apply with approval gating and loopback refusal
- document the new doctor workflow and add focused coverage for the skill, wrapper, CLI routing, gateway dispatcher, and repair tool

## Why
The PRD requires a least-privilege operator workflow that can diagnose locally in readonly mode, preview repairs without mutation, and only execute repairs through a narrow approved remote path.

## Affected subsystems
- doctor CLI and repair flows
- bundled skills and sandbox skill mirroring
- gateway protocol, method registration, and control-plane rate limiting
- agent tool registration and approval-bound remote execution
- operator docs for doctor and skills

## Security and design notes
- triage is constrained to a fixed readonly wrapper with an allowlisted evidence command set
- repair execution is limited to the dedicated `doctor.run` RPC and refuses local mode and direct loopback remote targets
- runtime approval remains the hard gate for remote preview/apply actions
- `doctor.run` returns structured results only; it does not expose a generic remote shell or raw command passthrough
- `apply` is intentionally launched in the background so the RPC can respond before a gateway restart interrupts the current session

## Risks / blast radius
- doctor flow changes touch config normalization, state integrity checks, UI freshness repair, sandbox repair, gateway service repair, and daemon/bootstrap handling
- dry-run preview correctness depends on every mutating path being represented accurately; missed mutation sites would be the main regression risk
- the new remote repair tool depends on existing gateway approval and remote credent

items.author: wsj0415

items.title: feat: --resolve-replies flag inlines referenced message on history/watch
items.content:
## Motivation

LLM agents running on top of `imsg watch` have a recurring failure mode: when a user sends a native iMessage reply (or a thread reply) the agent sees two blobs of text back-to-back — the quoted context and the new message — with no structural separator. When the quoted context happens to be the agent's own prior message, models read it as "my own message came back to me" and loop or re-send.

Downstream consumers work around this with bash wrappers that run a second `sqlite3` process against `chat.db` to look up `reply_to_guid` and prepend a synthetic `[Reply to #N: ...]` line. That adds a process per event, runs an unpooled connection, and only covers one of the two reply mechanisms (`reply_to_guid` misses `thread_originator_guid`, which is what most users actually hit on modern iOS/macOS).

This change lets `imsg` itself resolve the referenced message in the same connection that's already open, with a single bounded `SELECT`, and emit it as structured data.

## Changes

- **`MessageStore.messageByGUID(_:)`** — new public API on `IMsgCore`. Single bounded `SELECT ... WHERE m.guid = ? LIMIT 1`, mirrors the existing `messages()` decode path (attributedBody, audio transcription, sender fallback, reaction metadata). Returns `nil` for empty/whitespace/unknown GUIDs, doesn't throw.
- **`--resolve-replies` flag** on `imsg history` and `imsg watch`. Opt-in, so the baseline JSON shape is unchanged for consumers that don't pass it.
- **JSON mode** adds two optional nested fields:
  - `reply_to` — present when `reply_to_guid` resolves. Object: `{id, guid, sender, is_from_me, text, created_at}`
  - `thread_originator` — present when `thread_originator_guid` is distinct from `reply_to_guid` and resolves. Same shape.
  - Failed lookups (GUID not in DB) omit the nested field but still emit the outer message.
- **Text mode** prefixes a `  ↳ reply to <sender> #<id>: <excerpt>` line (or `↳ thread to …` when only `thread_originator_guid` is present) before the replying
items.author: lesaai

items.title: chore: upgrade openclaw to hiclaw-2026.4.14 and fix Manager Matrix userId
items.content:
## Summary

- Bumps the `openclaw-base` image's bundled OpenClaw from `hiclaw-v1.1` (`86dad13`) to `hiclaw-2026.4.14` (`b181285`). The new dependency includes the matrix SSRF auto-allow for private/loopback homeservers (`http://hiclaw-controller:6167` and similar) and a `curl` retry fallback for the `matrix-sdk-crypto-nodejs` native addon.
- Re-applies commit `3f8f84b` ("fix: use 'manager' as Matrix username in DeployManagerConfig") which the controller refactor in `d029ed2` accidentally reverted. With the buggy `WorkerName: req.Name`, the controller was generating `userId=@<crName>:<domain>` (typically `@default:matrix-local.hiclaw.io:18080`) and pushing it to MinIO, where `mc mirror` would later overwrite the manager-rendered `openclaw.json`. OpenClaw's Matrix client then filtered every admin DM to the wrong localpart, so the agent silently received zero messages even though the gateway was healthy and the access token was valid for `@manager`.
- Hardens `manager-openclaw.json.tmpl` and `start-manager-agent.sh` to explicitly write `channels.matrix.userId=@manager:<domain>` and `channels.matrix.network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork=true`, and to stamp `meta.lastTouchedVersion` plus mirror to `openclaw.json.bak` so OpenClaw's `config-observe-recovery` does not clobber the edits as `missing-meta-vs-last-good`.

## Why this matters

Without this PR, `test-02-create-worker` fails with `LLM Calls: 0` on the new OpenClaw release because the Manager never sees the admin's create-worker DM — the failure is silent (gateway healthy, sync running, token valid for `@manager`) and only visible by inspecting `channels.matrix.userId` in `/root/manager-workspace/openclaw.json` versus the `whoami` result for the access token.

## Test plan

- [x] `make build-openclaw-base build-manager build-worker build-copaw-worker build-embedded` — green locally with `OPENCLAW_BASE_IMAGE=hiclaw/openclaw-base OPENCLAW_BASE_VERSION=latest`
- [x] `SKIP_BUILD=1 make install-embedded` — controller +
